hook: Brand-new landed vs resale: who's blinking first on price?

slides.sub: New launches hold firm while resale owners quietly shave asking prices. Here's where the give is.

slides.lead: Two-speed landed market: new stock holds ASP, resale is where deals are being made.
slides.source: URA caveats · SRX · POV Guy analysis

slides.pre: Resale landed avg discount to last ask
slides.post: Approx. 30-day rolling median across D10, D11, D19 terrace and semi-D resale caveats. New launches: 0% off list.
slides.source: URA caveats · SRX Apr 2026 · POV Guy

slides.title: Three things the discount gap tells you

items.body: Resale owners who bought pre-2022 still have equity buffer — they can shave 6% and stay green.
New-launch developers are holding ASP because land cost locked in at peak; no room to cut without signal damage.
Gap between new and resale psf is the widest since 2019 — patient buyers in D10/D11 have rare leverage right now.

slides.source: URA caveats · SRX · POV Guy analysis

slides.title: Districts with most resale landed activity

items.label: District 19
items.sub: Serangoon / Hougang — high terrace volume, fastest churn

items.label: District 10
items.sub: Bukit Timah — semi-D / bungalow, buyers negotiating hard

items.label: District 11
items.sub: Thomson / Novena — inter-terrace cluster, steady enquiries

items.label: District 28
items.sub: Seletar / Yio Chu Kang — new-launch GCB fringe, firm pricing

slides.source: URA caveats · SRX · POV Guy analysis

slides.label: Where the real trade is
slides.punch: When new launches won't move on price but resale is quietly 6% off ask, the arbitrage is obvious. The risk isn't buying resale — it's waiting for a 'better' market that may not come in D10/D11.
slides.punch_bold: Resale landed at a negotiated price beats new-launch landed at a fixed one.

slides.title: Where sellers are still transacting

items.body: D19 terrace: highest resale caveat count this month — motivated sellers, shorter days-on-market vs D10.
D10 semi-D: fewer deals but bigger quantum — buyers using longer due-diligence window to extract 5–8% off ask.
New launches islandwide: caveats thin, pricing unchanged — developers absorbing holding cost rather than discounting.

slides.source: URA caveats · SRX Apr 2026 · POV Guy

slides.pre: Eyeing landed in D10, D11 or D19?
slides.title: Let's map your
slides.sub: 30-min strategy call. I'll show you which resale listings have real room, which new launches are worth the premium, and where to bid first.

slides.title: Save this before
slides.sub: Forward to anyone comparing new-launch vs resale landed right now. Knowing the discount range before you walk in changes everything.

caption:
📉 Resale landed is quietly 6% off ask — new launches haven't moved an inch.

• Resale sellers in D10, D11 & D19 are trimming 4–8% off original asking price
• New-launch developers are holding list price — land cost gives them no room to cut
• The new-vs-resale psf gap is the widest it's been since 2019

If you're buying landed, the leverage is in resale right now — not the showroom.
